================================================================================
FULCRUM PROJECT - KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY
================================================================================

DEEP RESEARCH COMPLETED - See detailed analysis in:
- /mnt/c/Users/autom/VS Code GitHub Repos/DevTeam6/FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md (860 lines)
- /mnt/c/Users/autom/VS Code GitHub Repos/DevTeam6/FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md (350 lines)

================================================================================
1. WHAT IS FULCRUM?
================================================================================

✓ AI-powered command center for shipping software
✓ Orchestrates multiple agents (Claude Code, OpenCode) in parallel
✓ Single-user workflow tool designed for solopreneurs + CTOs
✓ Version: 4.7.1 (React 19 + Hono on Bun)
✓ Licensed under PolyForm Perimeter 1.0.0 (can build/sell software using it)

Key Tagline: "Harness Attention. Orchestrate Agents. Leverage Your Time Wisely."

================================================================================
2. ARCHITECTURE - 3 LAYER STACK
================================================================================

FRONTEND (React 19 + TanStack Router)
├── Kanban board (5 status columns: TO_DO, IN_PROGRESS, IN_REVIEW, DONE, CANCELED)
├── Terminal tabs (xterm.js for agent I/O)
├── Task details + dependencies visualization
├── Calendar + messaging + monitoring
└── Chat UI (Claude + OpenCode integrated)

BACKEND (Hono on Bun + SQLite + Drizzle ORM)
├── REST API (/api/*)
├── WebSocket multiplexing (/ws/terminal)
├── MCP HTTP endpoint (/mcp)
├── 100+ MCP tools across 20 categories
├── Multi-service support (calendar, messaging, notifications)
└── Terminal + PTY management (dtach-backed, persistent sessions)

DATABASE (SQLite, single-file, portable)
├── 30+ tables covering: tasks, terminals, agents, messaging, calendars
├── FTS5 full-text search (memory, conversations, tasks)
├── Task dependencies, recurrence, tags, attachments
└── Chat sessions, messages, artifacts (from assistant conversations)

================================================================================
3. KANBAN SYSTEM - FULLY IMPLEMENTED
================================================================================

Task Status Workflow:
  TO_DO → IN_PROGRESS → IN_REVIEW → DONE
                    ↘ CANCELED ↙

Task Types:
  • WORKTREE: Git-isolated work (separate branch per task)
  • SCRATCH: Directory-isolated work (non-git, for scripts/analysis)
  • MANUAL: Tracking-only (no agent, no directory)

Task Properties:
  ✓ Title, description, priority (high/medium/low)
  ✓ Status, position (in column), pinned (to top)
  ✓ Due date (color-coded: red=overdue, orange=today, yellow=tomorrow)
  ✓ Time estimate (hours)
  ✓ Tags (M:M relationship, reusable across tasks/projects)
  ✓ Dependencies (task relationships - depends_on, relates_to, subtask)
  ✓ Links (PRs, issues, docs, design files)
  ✓ Attachments (file uploads, 50MB max)
  ✓ Recurrence (daily/weekly/biweekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly)
  ✓ Agent assignment (claude, opencode, or manual)
  ✓ Project association (nullable - null = "Inbox")

Kanban Board Features:
  ✓ Drag-and-drop between columns
  ✓ Bulk operations (select multiple → move/delete/tag)
  ✓ Filtering by: project, tags, status, date range, search
  ✓ Calendar integration (see due dates on calendar)
  ✓ Dependency graph visualization (interactive)
  ✓ Full-text search across all task content
  ✓ Recurrence handling (auto-create next task when marked DONE)

Key Files:
  • Schema: server/db/schema.ts (tasks, taskRelationships, taskLinks, taskAttachments)
  • Services: server/services/task-service.ts, server/services/task-status.ts
  • API: server/routes/tasks.ts
  • Frontend: frontend/routes/tasks/, frontend/components/kanban/

================================================================================
4. AGENT WORKSPACES - PER-TASK ISOLATION COMPLETE
================================================================================

Isolation Mechanism:
  
  WORKTREE TASKS:
    ~/.fulcrum/repos/{repo-path}/.git/worktrees/
    └── {task-id}-{branch-name}/
        ├── .git (separate git state)
        ├── src/, package.json, etc.
        └── [Claude/OpenCode runs here]
  
  SCRATCH TASKS:
    ~/.fulcrum/scratch/{task-id}/
    ├── (free-form file structure)
    └── [Agent can work with any files]

Terminal Management:
  • Each task gets unique PTY (pseudo-terminal)
  • Backed by dtach socket (survives server restart)
  • Stored in: terminals table (id, cwd, tmuxSession, status, exitCode)
  • Organized in tabs: terminalTabs (persistent tab structure)
  • UI state: terminalViewState (singleton, tracks active tab + focused terminals)

Workspace Persistence:
  ✓ Session survives Fulcrum server restart (dtach backing)
  ✓ Can close laptop, agent keeps working on remote server
  ✓ SSH port forwarding recommended (http://localhost:7777)

Configuration Per Workspace:
  • Task-level overrides: agentOptions, opencodeModel
  • Repo-level defaults: defaultAgent, claudeOptions, opencodeOptions
  • Project-level defaults: defaultAgent, claudeOptions, opencodeOptions
  • Global defaults: settings (fnox)
  • Hierarchy: Task > Repo > Project > Global

Key Files:
  • Terminal: server/terminal/ (pty-manager.ts, dtach-service.ts, tab-manager.ts)
  • PTY: server/terminal/pty-manager.ts
  • WebSocket: server/websocket/terminal-ws.ts
  • Database: server/db/schema.ts (terminals, terminalTabs, terminalViewState)

================================================================================
5. CLAUDE CODE INTEGRATION - COMPLETE
================================================================================

Plugin Location: plugins/fulcrum/ (skills, hooks, commands)

Status Sync Mechanism:
  Claude stops (idle event)
    ↓ Plugin Stop hook fires
    ↓ Runs: fulcrum current-task review
    ↓ Auto-detects task from worktree path
    ↓ HTTP POST /api/tasks/{id}/status → IN_REVIEW
    ↓ Task moves to IN_REVIEW column in Fulcrum
    ↓ Notification sent to user (if configured)

User Responds → Plugin UserPromptSubmit hook fires → fulcrum current-task in-progress

MCP Tool Access:
  ✓ Claude calls MCP tools via HTTP /mcp endpoint
  ✓ 100+ tools available (create_task, move_task, send_notification, etc.)
  ✓ No context bloat: core tools only (~8), others discovered via search_tools

Key Integration Points:
  • Plugin hooks: plugins/fulcrum/hooks/hooks.json
  • Skill documentation: plugins/fulcrum/skills/fulcrum/SKILL.md
  • Commands: plugins/fulcrum/commands/ (notify, pr, task-info)
  • Service: server/services/assistant-service.ts
  • Routes: server/routes/assistant.ts
  • MCP tools: cli/src/mcp/tools/tasks.ts (move_task, create_task, etc.)

Installation:
  claude plugin marketplace add knowsuchagency/fulcrum
  claude plugin install fulcrum@fulcrum --scope user

================================================================================
6. OPENCODE INTEGRATION - COMPLETE
================================================================================

Plugin Location: plugins/fulcrum-opencode/index.ts

SDK Integration:
  • Auto-starts OpenCode server on localhost:4096 if not running
  • Maintains client singleton
  • Maps Fulcrum session ID ↔ OpenCode SDK session ID

Status Tracking:
  • Monitors events: chat.message, session.idle, session.status, tool.execute
  • Activity tracking: recordActivity() prevents false idle
  • Debounces status changes (500ms) to avoid thrashing
  • Subagent support: ignores subagent idles, only main session controls task status

Event Flow:
  User input → setStatus("in-progress")
  OpenCode idle (1.5s debounce) → scheduleIdleTransition() → setStatus("review")

Chat Service:
  • Location: server/services/opencode-chat-service.ts
  • Lazy-loads OpenCode client on first message
  • Builds page-context system prompts
  • Streams responses via SSE
  • Supports attachments (images, files)

Installation:
  fulcrum opencode install

Key Difference from Claude:
  • OpenCode uses SDK (not CLI hooks)
  • Supports model selection (provider/model format)
  • Lower cost (uses other LLM providers, not just Anthropic)
  • Same MCP tool access as Claude

================================================================================
7. MCP (MODEL CONTEXT PROTOCOL) IMPLEMENTATION - 100+ TOOLS
================================================================================

Tool Categories (20+):
  ✓ core (search_tools, list_tasks, etc.)
  ✓ tasks (CRUD, dependencies, links, attachments)
  ✓ projects (CRUD, links, attachments)
  ✓ repositories (management, scanning)
  ✓ apps (Docker Compose deployment)
  ✓ filesystem (browse, read, write)
  ✓ exec (shell commands with persistent sessions)
  ✓ notifications (multi-channel)
  ✓ settings (config read/update)
  ✓ backup (database snapshot/restore)
  ✓ email (Gmail management)
  ✓ messaging (WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Slack)
  ✓ assistant (chat session management)
  ✓ caldav (calendar sync, event management)
  ✓ gmail (Google account management)
  ✓ memory (knowledge store, master file)
  ✓ jobs (systemd/launchd timer management)
  ✓ search (unified FTS5 full-text search)

Transport Modes:
  1. HTTP (stateless per-request) - POST /mcp → used by external agents
  2. Stdio (persistent) - fulcrum mcp → used by CLI
  3. HTTP Restricted - POST /mcp/observer → observer-mode tools only

Deferred Loading:
  • Core tools: always loaded (~8 tools)
  • Deferred tools: discovered via search_tools (~92 tools)
  • Benefit: reduces token budget, prevents context bloat
  • Pattern: Agent calls search_tools("task"), then can use returned tools

Key Files:
  • Registry: cli/src/mcp/registry.ts (tool metadata, categories, keywords)
  • Tools: cli/src/mcp/tools/ (20+ modules)
  • Server (stdio): cli/src/mcp/index.ts
  • Server (HTTP): server/routes/mcp.ts
  • Client: cli/src/client.ts (HTTP client for /api/* calls)

================================================================================
8. KEY EXTENSION POINTS FOR OMEGA
================================================================================

GOOD NEWS: Fulcrum is already designed for multiple agents!

1. AGENT PROVIDER ABSTRACTION (Recommended Pattern)
   
   Create: server/services/agent-provider.ts
   
   interface AgentProvider {
     name: string
     startSession(options: SessionOptions): Promise<AgentSession>
     sendMessage(sessionId: string, message: string): Promise<void>
     onStatusChange(handler: (status: TaskStatus) => void): void
   }
   
   Implementations: ClaudeProvider, OpencodeProvider, OmegaProvider

2. STATUS TRACKING SERVICE (Already Exists for Claude/OpenCode)
   
   Pattern: Monitor agent events → map to task status
   Can generalize: server/services/agent-status-tracker.ts
   
   Works by: Plugin hooks / SDK events / MCP tool calls
   All methods already supported by Fulcrum

3. PLUGIN HOOK SYSTEM (Already Exists)
   
   Claude: Stop hook, UserPromptSubmit hook
   OpenCode: SDK event listeners
   Omega: Similar hooks or SDK pattern
   
   All hooks eventually call: fulcrum current-task {status}

4. MCP TOOLS ARE ALREADY AGENT-AGNOSTIC
   
   ✓ No hardcoded Claude/OpenCode references in tools
   ✓ FulcrumClient makes HTTP calls back to /api/*
   ✓ Works for any agent that can call HTTP
   ✓ Just add Omega to supported agent types in schema

5. TERMINAL ISOLATION ALREADY GENERIC
   
   ✓ PTY manager doesn't care which agent runs in terminal
   ✓ Works for any agent with shell access
   ✓ Already supports worktrees + scratch dirs

6. CONFIGURATION HIERARCHY READY
   
   ✓ agent field: 'claude' | 'opencode' → add 'omega'
   ✓ agentOptions: JSON → store omega-specific flags
   ✓ Settings already have agent.defaultAgent
   ✓ Just add agent.omegaOptions to settings schema

INTEGRATION CHECKLIST:
  □ Create OmegaChatService (echo OpenCodeChatService pattern)
  □ Add 'omega' to agent enum in schema
  □ Add agent detection to assistant routes (like OpenCode check)
  □ Create status tracker (plugin hooks or SDK pattern)
  □ Optional: Create Omega plugin (plugins/omega/)
  □ Update settings schema with omegaOptions

NO MAJOR CHANGES NEEDED - Fulcrum was designed for this!

================================================================================
9. CRITICAL FILES & ENTRY POINTS
================================================================================

SERVER STARTUP:
  server/index.ts - Main entry point
    ├── initPTYManager() - Terminal management
    ├── createApp() - Hono app
    ├── startMessagingChannels() - WhatsApp, Discord, etc.
    ├── startAssistantScheduler() - Daily rituals, sweeps
    ├── startPRMonitor() - GitHub integration
    ├── startCaldavSync() - Calendar sync
    └── serve() on port 7777

APP SETUP:
  server/app.ts - Route mounting
    ├── /api/tasks/*
    ├── /api/assistant/*
    ├── /api/projects/*
    ├── /api/repositories/*
    ├── /api/search
    ├── /api/memory*
    ├── /api/messaging/*
    ├── /mcp (MCP HTTP)
    ├── /ws/terminal (WebSocket)
    └── Frontend (SPA)

KANBAN BOARD:
  frontend/routes/tasks/ - Task pages
  frontend/components/kanban/ - Board components

AGENT INTEGRATION:
  server/services/assistant-service.ts - Claude + OpenCode
  server/services/opencode-chat-service.ts - OpenCode SDK
  server/routes/assistant.ts - Chat API
  plugins/fulcrum/ - Claude plugin
  plugins/fulcrum-opencode/ - OpenCode plugin

MCP TOOLS:
  cli/src/mcp/ - Tool registration
  cli/src/mcp/registry.ts - Tool metadata
  cli/src/mcp/tools/ - 20+ tool modules
  server/routes/mcp.ts - HTTP transport
  server/routes/mcp-restricted.ts - Observer mode

DATABASE:
  server/db/schema.ts - 30+ tables
  drizzle/migrations/ - SQL migrations
  ~/.fulcrum/fulcrum.db - SQLite database

CONFIGURATION:
  server/lib/settings/fnox.ts - Config wrapper
  server/lib/settings/types.ts - Settings schema
  ~/.fulcrum/.fnox.toml - Single source of truth
  ~/.fulcrum/age.txt - Encryption key

================================================================================
10. TECH STACK SUMMARY
================================================================================

Frontend:
  • React 19
  • TanStack Router (file-based routing)
  • TanStack Query (server state)
  • TanStack Virtual (virtualization)
  • shadcn/ui v4 (UI components)
  • xterm.js (terminal emulation)
  • Atlaskit Pragmatic DnD (drag-and-drop)
  • Dagre (dependency graph visualization)
  • Recharts (system metrics charts)

Backend:
  • Hono (web framework on Bun)
  • Bun (JavaScript runtime)
  • SQLite + Drizzle ORM
  • bun-pty (PTY management)
  • Model Context Protocol SDK

Integrations:
  • Claude Agent SDK (v0.2.72)
  • OpenCode SDK (v1.1.34)
  • Baileys (WhatsApp)
  • Discord.js
  • Telegram Bot API
  • Slack Bolt
  • Google APIs (Calendar, Gmail)
  • CalDAV
  • fnox (encrypted config)
  • age (encryption)

Build:
  • Vite
  • TypeScript 5.9
  • Mise (task runner)
  • Drizzle Kit (migrations)

================================================================================
11. DEPLOYMENT MODES
================================================================================

Development:
  mise run dev              # Frontend + backend dev servers
  Uses http://localhost:5173 (frontend) → proxies to localhost:7777 (backend)

Production (CLI):
  npx @knowsuchagency/fulcrum@latest up     # Start daemon
  npx @knowsuchagency/fulcrum@latest down   # Stop daemon

Desktop App:
  Download DMG (macOS) or AppImage (Linux)
  Bundles server executable (no Bun dependency needed)

Remote Server:
  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/knowsuchagency/fulcrum/main/install.sh | bash
  SSH port forwarding: ssh -L 7777:localhost:7777 your-server

Production Deployment:
  Docker Compose (with Traefik + Cloudflare DNS/tunnels)
  Auto-deploy on git changes (git-watcher)

================================================================================
12. DATABASE SCHEMA (30+ Tables)
================================================================================

Core:
  • tasks - Task CRUD + status + agent + recurrence
  • taskRelationships - Dependencies (replaces taskDependencies)
  • taskLinks - URL references (PR, issue, design doc, etc.)
  • taskAttachments - File uploads
  • taskTags - M:M relationship between tasks and tags
  • tags - Reusable labels

Projects & Repos:
  • projects - Project entity
  • projectRepositories - 1:N join table
  • projectTags - M:M relationship
  • projectLinks - URL references
  • projectAttachments - File uploads
  • repositories - Git repos with agent config
  • apps - Docker Compose applications
  • appServices - Services within apps
  • deployments - Deployment history
  • tunnels - Cloudflare Tunnels

Agents & Chat:
  • chatSessions - Conversations (provider: 'claude'|'opencode'|'omega')
  • chatMessages - Message history
  • artifacts - Generated content (charts, markdown, code)
  • sweepRuns - Observer/scheduler runs (internal)

Terminal:
  • terminals - PTY sessions
  • terminalTabs - Tab organization
  • terminalViewState - UI state (singleton)

Messaging:
  • messagingConnections - Channel auth state
  • messagingSessionMappings - User→session mapping
  • channelMessages - All channel messages (unified)

Calendar:
  • caldavAccounts - Account credentials
  • caldavCalendars - Synced calendars
  • caldavEvents - Calendar events
  • caldavCopyRules - Auto-copy rules between calendars
  • caldavCopiedEvents - Track copied events (avoid duplicates)
  • googleAccounts - Google API accounts (Calendar + Gmail)
  • gmailDrafts - Gmail draft cache

Memory & Search:
  • memories - FTS5 searchable knowledge
  • observerInvocations - Message processing tracking

Monitoring:
  • systemMetrics - CPU/memory/disk history

================================================================================
13. CONFIGURATION (80+ Settings in .fnox.toml)
================================================================================

Sections:
  • server - Port configuration
  • paths - Default directories
  • editor - VS Code / Cursor / Windsurf / Zed integration
  • integrations - GitHub, Cloudflare, Google OAuth credentials
  • agent - AI agent defaults (Claude, OpenCode, Omega)
  • tasks - Task creation defaults
  • appearance - UI theme, language
  • assistant - Model, provider, observer settings
  • caldav - Calendar sync configuration
  • notifications - Multi-channel notification settings
  • zai - z.ai integration settings

Encryption:
  • Sensitive values: API keys, tokens, webhook URLs
  • Encrypted with age (in ~/.fulcrum/age.txt)
  • Non-sensitive values: readable via fnox export

Precedence:
  Environment variable > fnox setting > built-in default

================================================================================
14. PERFORMANCE & DESIGN PATTERNS
================================================================================

Terminal I/O:
  • WebSocket multiplexing (single connection handles multiple terminals)
  • TTY buffer management (prevents memory leaks)
  • Optimistic client updates (tempId → realId transitions)

Task Updates:
  • Broadcast via WebSocket on status change
  • Kanban board re-renders in real-time
  • All connected clients see updates simultaneously

MCP Tool Discovery:
  • Deferred loading (only core tools in context)
  • search_tools returns metadata (small payload)
  • Agent discovers tools on demand

Message Processing:
  • Async (doesn't block API responses)
  • Circuit breaker on observers (prevents API abuse)
  • Aggregate stats tracking (success/error rates)

Agent Idle Detection:
  • Claude: Plugin hooks (Stop event)
  • OpenCode: SDK event listeners + debouncing (1.5s)
  • Omega: TBD (can mirror OpenCode pattern)

================================================================================
15. SECURITY & LIMITS
================================================================================

File Uploads:
  • MIME type whitelist (PDF, images, docs, spreadsheets, etc.)
  • 50MB size limit per file
  • Stored in ~/.fulcrum/uploads/tasks/{taskId}/

API Rate Limiting:
  • No explicit rate limiting (single-user tool)
  • Observer mode uses circuit breaker to prevent abuse

Secret Encryption:
  • age encryption (modern, auditable)
  • Key in ~/.fulcrum/age.txt (never committed)
  • Environment variables can override

Input Validation:
  • SQL injection prevention (Drizzle ORM)
  • Path traversal prevention (normalized paths)
  • XSS prevention (React escaping)

================================================================================
16. NEXT STEPS FOR OMEGA INTEGRATION
================================================================================

RECOMMENDED APPROACH:

1. Copy OpenCode pattern:
   • server/services/opencode-chat-service.ts → omega-chat-service.ts
   • plugins/fulcrum-opencode/index.ts → plugins/omega/index.ts
   • Replace OpenCode SDK with Omega SDK

2. Update schema:
   • Add 'omega' to agent enum
   • Add 'omega' to provider enum
   • Create omegaOptions field in settings

3. Update routes:
   • server/routes/assistant.ts - Add omega provider check
   • Reuse existing streaming/message infrastructure

4. Create MCP tools:
   • Omega can use existing 100+ tools
   • No changes needed to tool registry

5. Status tracking:
   • Create agent-status-tracker.ts
   • Register hooks or SDK callbacks
   • Call fulcrum current-task {status}

TOTAL EFFORT: ~3-4 days for basic integration
• Day 1: Create OmegaChatService + routes
• Day 2: Database + settings schema
• Day 3: Status tracking + plugin
• Day 4: Testing + documentation

ALREADY SUPPORTED (no work needed):
  ✓ Terminal isolation (worktrees + scratch)
  ✓ Task CRUD (all MCP tools)
  ✓ Message persistence (chatSessions)
  ✓ Memory store (master file + FTS5)
  ✓ Notifications (multi-channel)
  ✓ Calendar (CalDAV + Google)
  ✓ Messaging (WhatsApp, Discord, etc.)
  ✓ File attachments
  ✓ Task dependencies
  ✓ Project management
  ✓ And 80+ more features

================================================================================
SUMMARY
================================================================================

Fulcrum is a COMPLETE, PRODUCTION-READY platform for AI agent orchestration.

✓ Fully-implemented kanban system with task dependencies, recurrence, and metadata
✓ Per-task agent workspaces (worktrees + scratch dirs)
✓ Two agents already integrated (Claude Code + OpenCode) with proven patterns
✓ 100+ MCP tools with deferred loading (minimal context bloat)
✓ Messaging, memory, calendar, notifications all built-in
✓ Database migrations, config management, logging ready
✓ React + Hono stack is modern and maintainable

KEY ARCHITECTURAL STRENGTH:
  The system was explicitly designed for MULTIPLE AGENTS.
  Claude and OpenCode are proof-of-concept implementations.
  Adding Omega requires ~1000 LOC, no major refactoring needed.

Documentation generated in:
  • FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md (14,000+ words)
  • FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md (5,000+ words)

Ready for Omega agent orchestration system design!

================================================================================
